title: Measurement of Left Ventricular Performance in Langendorff Perfused Mouse Hearts
author: E. Dale Abel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7tfhnjn
materials: Left ventricle systolic pressure (LVSP): Units mmHg. (
Heart Rate (HR): Units beats per minute (

steps.title: Hearts are isolated and the aorta...
steps.instruction: Hearts are isolated and the aorta is cannulated using a 20g steel cannula. Hearts are perfused at a constant pressure of 60 mmHg by an aortic cannula delivering warm (37°C) Krebs buffer containing (in mM) 118 NaCl, 4.7 KCl, 25 NaHCO ³ , 1.2 MgSO 4 , 1.2 KH 2 PO 4 , 2 CaCl ² gassed with 95% O ² , 5% CO ² . Hearts are perfused with glucose 11mM as sole substrate or in combination with 1 or 1.2 mM palmitate. The pulmonary artery is transected to facilitate coronary venous drainage. A left ventricular polyethylene apical drain is inserted through a left atrial incision to allow thebesian venous drainage. Left ventricular pressure is monitored from a water-filled balloon placed through the left atrial appendage and connected to a Millar transducer. The volume of the balloon is adjusted to obtain a left ventricular diastolic pressure of 7 mmHg. Heart rates are adjusted to 360 beats/min by pacing at 6 Hz at the level of the atria.

steps.title: Left ventricle developed pressure (LVDP or...
steps.instruction: Left ventricle developed pressure (LVDP or LVDevP), which is LVSP – LV Diastolic pressure: Units mmHg. (

steps.title: Rate Pressure Product (RPP), which is LVDP x HR
steps.instruction: Rate Pressure Product (RPP), which is LVDP x HR. Units mmHg/sec (

steps.title: dP/dtmin and dP/dtmax, which are the...
steps.instruction: dP/dtmin and dP/dtmax, which are the maximal rates of LV pressure decay and LV, pressure development respectively. Units mmHg/msec. (

steps.title: Coronary Flow (ml/min)
steps.instruction: Coronary Flow (ml/min). This is determined by measuring the coronary effluent from the perfused heart.

steps.title: Coronary effluent is sampled from the...
steps.instruction: Coronary effluent is sampled from the pulmonary artery using a capillary tube. Oxygen content was measured using an Oxygen foxy probe (OceanOptics) and calculated using the formula: Where O ² solubility and O ² density are 23.9 μl/ml and 0.03933 μmol/μl respectively in a solution at 37°C respectively. Cardiac efficiency is calculated as the ratio of RPP/MVO ² expressed as a percentage.
